technology.name: VHS
technology.description: VHS was a home video cassette format that allowed users to record and watch video content on television.

technology.name: DVD
technology.description: DVD introduced digital video and audio quality on optical discs, replacing many VHS tapes.

technology.name: Streaming
technology.description: Streaming allows users to watch films, series and videos online without using physical media.

technology.name: Radio
technology.description: Radio made audio broadcasting available to mass audiences through scheduled live transmission.

technology.name: Spotify
technology.description: Spotify changed music consumption by offering on-demand songs, playlists and personalized recommendations.

technology.name: Analog Photography
technology.description: Analog photography uses film rolls and chemical development to capture and produce images.

technology.name: Digital Photography
technology.description: Digital photography stores images as digital files, making editing, storage and sharing easier.

technology.name: AI Generated Images
technology.description: AI generated images are created from text prompts or data using artificial intelligence models.
